data.title: Visa authorizes $500M deposit into U.S. litigation escrow account
data.description:
Visa (NYSE:V) has authorized the deposit of $500M into the U.S. litigation escrow account previously created under the payment network's U.S. retrospective responsibility plan.

Under the plan's terms, when Visa (NYSE:V) funds the account, the value of the company's B-1 and B-2 common stock, which are held mostly by financial institutions, are subject to dilution through downward adjustments to the conversion rates of the class B-1 to class A common stock and class B-2 to class A common stock.

The deposit and the adjustments to the class B-1 and B-2 conversion rates will be conducted in accordance with the company’s certificate of incorporation currently in effect, it said in a Securities and Exchange Commission filing.

V shares slipped 0.1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: DoubleDown Interactive registers 4.2M ADSs for resale by selling shareholder
data.description:
• DoubleDown Interactive (NASDAQ:DDI) filed a prospectus for the resale of up to 4.21M American Depositary Shares by STIC Special Situation Diamond Limited.
  • The ADSs represent up to 210,632.75 common shares, with each ADS equivalent to 0.05 of a common share.
  • DoubleDown is not selling any shares in this offering and will not receive proceeds from the sale.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: 1911 Gold announces upsize of “best efforts” life offering and private placement to C$17M
data.description:
• 1911 Gold (TSXV:AUMB:CA) on Friday said it has entered into an amended and restated agreement with Haywood Securities to amend the terms of its “best efforts” private placement and LIFE offering to increase the size of the offering to C$17 million.
  • Under the terms of the upsized offering, the agents have agreed to sell, on a "best efforts" private placement basis, up to 3.18M common shares which qualify as “flow-through shares” at a price of C$0.64 per CEE LIFE share and 14.8M common shares which qualify as “flow-through shares” at a price of C$0.554 per CDE Offered Share for aggregate gross proceeds to the company from the sale of CEE LIFE Shares and CDE Offered Shares of up to C$10.23M.
  • Additionally, the agents have agreed to sell, on a “best-efforts” private placement basis, up to 6.8M common shares at a price per Non-FT Share of C$0.45 and 5.65M common shares that qualify as “flow-through shares” at the CEE issue price for aggregate gross proceeds to the company from the sale of the Non-FT Shares and CEE PP shares of up to C$6,72M.
  • The marketed offering combines aggregate gross proceeds to the company of up to C$16.95M.
  • The net proceeds from the sale of the Non-FT Shares shall be used for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The upsized offering is expected to close on or about Oct 15.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Four Corners Property Trust buys Left Lane Auto property in Mississippi for $3 Million
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT) said on Friday it acquired a newly constructed auto service property leased to Left Lane Auto in Mississippi for $3 million.

  • The property, located in a strong retail corridor, is under a long-term triple net lease with about 15 years remaining.
  • Left Lane Auto operates 67 auto repair shops across 13 states.

  • The deal was priced at a 7.2% capitalization rate on rent, excluding transaction costs.

  • FCPT -0.03% after hours to $25.29.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Snap closes in red after six straight sessions of gains post insider transaction filings
data.description:
Snap (NYSE:SNAP) stock closed 3.32% lower at $8.16 on Friday, September 19, after six straight sessions of gains.

The Snapchat-parent had disclosed four insider sell transactions yesterday post market close. The transactions include CFO Andersen Derek selling 33,871 class A shares in multiple transactions at a weighted average price per share of $7.56.

Chief Accounting Officer Rebecca Morrow sold class A shares worth $18.90K, while Chief Business Officer Ajit Mohan sold shares worth $208.54K.

Snap faces an inquiry from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission concerning the potentially negative impacts of AI chatbots on children and teens.

The FTC had issued orders to seven companies, including Snap, seeking information on how these firms measure, test, and monitor the potential negative impacts.

Nonetheless, the stock had been on a winning streak. The Santa Monica, CA-based technology company added 18.33% over the course of the last six trading days, with a 3.40% gain logged on the day of the FTC probe announcement.

Another notable rise was recorded on Thursday, September 18. The stock closed the day 5.63% higher at $8.44.

SNAP has lost ~24% year-to-date.

Seeking Alpha authors grade the stock as Buy. However, the sell-side analysts as well as the Quant Rating system see SNAP as Hold.

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count gains for third straight week, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. gained 3 to 542, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest weekly count, rising for the third week in a row for the first time since February.

Oil rigs rose by 2 to 418 during the week, the highest since July, while gas rigs remained flat at 118 and 6 rigs were classified as miscellaneous; the total rig count was down 46 rigs, or 7.8% compared to the same time last year.

Rigs targeting crude oil in the biggest U.S. basins all were unchanged in the past week, with the Permian Basin at 252, the Eagle Ford at 33, and the Williston Basin at 29.

In the DJ-Niobrara shale in Colorado, Wyoming, Nebraska and Kansas, drillers added 2 rigs this week, boosting the count to 11, the highest since April 2024.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Walmart faces questions linking lax third-party vetting to marketplace counterfeits -- update
data.description:
Updated 16:04ET with comment from Walmart. 

Shares of Walmart (NYSE:WMT) are on watch in the wake of a CNBC report alleging that the retail giant’s lax vetting process for third-party merchants has resulted in a proliferation of counterfeit products on its website.

A CNBC investigation into Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) marketplace uncovered “dozens” of third-party sellers who had stolen the identity of another business,” and subsequently sold fake health and beauty products online.

The network included an interview with a Walmart employee responsible for approving third-party applications. According to the employee, Walmart (NYSE:WMT) was initially strict on third-party applications, but over time pressured employees to relax their standards and approve more sellers due to increased competition from Amazon (AMZN).

She added that sellers who were frustrated with Amazon’s (AMZN) rigorous screening moved to Walmart (NYSE:WMT), which allowed them to list their products more easily. Data compiled by Marketplace Pulse found that during the period between 2019 and 2024, the number of sellers on Walmart’s marketplace exploded by 900%, overlapping with a period in which Amazon (AMZN) increased security controls and banned many sellers for selling counterfeits.

By accepting more risks, Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) U.S. e-commerce business has swelled to nearly $100B in annual sales, CNBC claims. “They wanted the business, so they were willing to take a chance on it,” the employee said to CNBC.

In a statement to CNBC, Walmart (WMT) said it enforces a “zero-tolerance policy for prohibited or noncompliant products and continues to invest in new tools and technologies to help ensure only trusted, legitimate items reach our customers.”

However, as part of its investigation into Walmart (WMT) and Amazon’s (AMZN) seller applications, CNBC found that Walmart (WMT) required less documentation and vetting to sign up for its marketplace and imposed fewer restrictions on the types of products that could be sold compared to its competitors like Amazon (AMZN). The network tested the authenticity of 20 items offered by third-party sellers that were impersonating legitimate businesses and found all 20 were counterfeit.

Update: "Counterfeiters threaten brands and marketplaces worldwide, undermining trust and integrity. At Walmart, we continuously strengthen our marketplace to protect customers and sellers. Even with over half a billion products on our platform, we take swift, decisive action to remove any counterfeit goods and bad actors who fail to meet our standard," a Walmart spokesperson said to Seeking Alpha. 

Shares of Walmart (WMT) closed more than 1% lower matching a move across the consumer staples sector (XLP). ETFs with an exposure to Walmart (WMT) include iShares Global Consumer Staples (KXI), Global X Purecap MSCI Consumer Staples (GXPS), and  Vanguard Consumer Staples Index Fund (VDC).

data.title: Lennar anticipates Q4 deliveries of 22,000–23,000 homes as company prioritizes margin stabilization
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lennar Corporation (LEN) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Stuart Miller, Co-CEO & Executive Chairman, opened by stating that Lennar's third quarter results reflected "the continued softening of market conditions and affordability through our third quarter. Sales volume was difficult to maintain and required additional incentives in order to achieve our expected pace and to avoid building excess inventory." Miller explained, "While our deliveries were just below our goal for the quarter and while we sold more homes than expected during the quarter, these accomplishments came at the expense of further deterioration of margin, which came down to 17.5%." He announced Lennar will "begin to ease back our delivery expectations for the fourth quarter and full year in order to relieve the pressure on sales and deliveries and help establish a floor on margin."

  • Miller stated, "We will reduce our delivery expectations for the fourth quarter to 22,000 to 23,000 homes, and we will reduce our full year expectation to 81,500 to 82,500 for the full year." He noted that Lennar is positioned with "a lower cost structure, efficient product offerings and strong market positions" to benefit from pent-up demand if rates moderate.

  • Miller introduced Katherine Martin as the new Chief Legal Officer, following the retirement of Mark Sustana.

  • Jonathan Jaffe, Co-CEO and President, described the company's focus on "consistent high-volume production by leveraging advanced technology throughout our homebuilding operations" and cited improvements such as "average response time to leads improved by 53% from our second quarter, reducing it to just 46 seconds," with technology driving an "8% quarter-over-quarter increase in appointments."

  • Diane Bessette, CFO & VP, reported, "For the third quarter, our Financial Services team had operating earnings of $177 million. The strong earnings were primarily driven from our mortgage business and were driven by a higher profit per loan as a result of higher secondary margins." She added, "We ended the quarter with $1.4 billion of cash and total liquidity of $5.1 billion."

Outlook

  • Bessette provided Q4 guidance: "We expect Q4 new orders to be in the range of 20,000 to 21,000 homes as we match production and sales paces. We anticipate our Q4 deliveries to be in the range of 22,000 to 23,000 homes with a continued focus on turning inventory into cash. Our Q4 average sales price on those deliveries should be about $380,000 to $390,000 and gross margin should be approximately 17.5%." She projected, "these estimates should produce an EPS range of approximately $2.10 to $2.30 per share for the quarter."

  • Miller emphasized, "We expect our margins will come in at approximately 17.5%, consistent with our last quarter, of course, depending on market conditions."

Financial Results

  • Miller reported Lennar "started approximately 21,500 homes. We delivered approximately 21,500 homes and sold just over 23,000 homes." He noted, "sales incentives rose to 14.3%, reducing our gross margin to 17.5%, which was lower than expected on a lower-than-expected average sales price of $383,000. Our SG&A came in at 8.2%, which produced a net margin of 9.2%."

  • Bessette stated, "With our focus on turning inventory, our inventory turn increased to 1.9x, and our return on inventory was 24%." She noted, "We ended the quarter owning 11,000 homesites and controlling 512,000 homesites for a total of 523,000 homesites."

  • Lennar repurchased 4.1 million shares for $507 million and paid $129 million in dividends. The company ended the quarter with stockholders' equity just under $23 billion and book value per share of about $89.

Q&A

  • Alan Ratner, Zelman & Associates LLC: Asked about the nature of the delivery pullback and whether incentives have already been reduced. Miller responded, "I wouldn't really look at it as a change in strategy. I would look at it more that we are making adjustments as we go forward...we're just recalibrating to make sure that we're not pushing too hard on a market that really doesn't want to be pushed." Ratner also questioned the flexibility of the land strategy. Miller replied, "We are not constrained in any way by our land relationships or the reconfiguration of land."

  • Stephen Kim, Evercore ISI: Probed on the duration of the "pause" in volume. Miller said, "we're running a marathon and partway through, we're just taking a moment to take a breath, let our body catch up."

  • Michael Rehaut, JPMorgan: Inquired if the gross margin floor dictated the slower volume. Miller answered, "we just felt it was a good time to take a little pressure off."

  • Susan Maklari, Goldman Sachs: Asked about inventory turns. Miller stated, "at the local level, that kind of North Star is very much a part of the discussion as we get cycle times down" and Jaffe added the company is making "good progress" on inventory turns.

  • John Lovallo, UBS: Asked about the slight miss in Q3 deliveries. Jaffe said, "It really is just timing and relative to when sales occur getting through the mortgage approval process, nothing more than that."

  • Jade Rahmani, KBW: Asked about Millrose contributions. Bessette said, "it's been about -- Dave, correct me if I'm wrong, but 25%-ish in that zone."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts demonstrated a neutral to slightly negative tone, pressing management on the strategic adjustment, underlying demand elasticity, and the impact of incentives. Questions were probing but not overtly skeptical.

  • Management maintained a neutral to slightly defensive tone, emphasizing that the adjustment is not a shift in strategy but a tactical response to market softness. Phrases like "we're just recalibrating" and "we're taking a moment to take a breath" indicate a cautious but confident approach.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone was more focused on the implications of the strategic adjustment, while management was more open in acknowledging near-term challenges but optimistic about the firm's position and flexibility.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter introduced a tactical pullback in volume and delivery expectations to stabilize margins, a change from the prior quarter's focus on maintaining volume despite margin pressure.

  • Delivery guidance for Q4 was lowered to 22,000–23,000 homes from an earlier expectation of 22,000–23,000, with full-year deliveries now expected at 81,500–82,500 homes versus the previous range of 86,000–88,000 homes.

  • Average sales price guidance was slightly expanded to $380,000–$390,000, while margin expectations were reduced to 17.5% from 18% previously.

  • The tone has shifted from an emphasis on technology-driven volume and efficiency to a more measured approach prioritizing margin protection and operational flexibility.

  • Analysts are more concerned about elasticity of demand and the sustainability of margins at lower volumes.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing affordability challenges, requiring increased sales incentives "to achieve our sales targets."

  • The company faces risk from higher interest rates and volatile consumer confidence, which have not yet translated into stronger sales despite recent rate declines.

  • Analysts expressed concern about demand elasticity, margin sustainability, and the implications of the Millrose land banking structure.

  • Management outlined mitigation efforts, including leveraging land banking flexibility, ongoing cost reductions, and a focus on technology and operational efficiency.

Final Takeaway

Lennar management communicated that the company's tactical pullback in deliveries is a response to persistent market softness and is intended to protect margins while maintaining operational flexibility. The company remains committed to its technology-driven, asset-light strategy and expects to benefit from improved demand if interest rates decline further. Management underscored its strong balance sheet and land banking relationships as enablers for future growth and shareholder value creation, positioning Lennar to respond to both near-term pressures and long-term opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Cheniere, Kinder Morgan, Targa Resources, Williams started with Outperform ratings at BMO
data.description:
BMO Capital initiated energy infrastructure stocks Cheniere Energy (NYSE:LNG), Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI), Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) and Williams (NYSE:WMB) with Outperform ratings, although all four stocks trade lower Friday as part of a broad decline in the energy sector as crude oil prices fell for a third straight day.

Cheniere (NYSE:LNG) is started at Outperform with a $268 price target, as BMO's Ameet Thakkar sees the company as a core long-term holding for energy infrastructure investors, backed by long-term take-or-pay contracts offering "exceptional earnings visibility (remaining average contract life 16 years) and downside protection."

Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI), initiated at Outperform and a $32 PT, looks well positioned for an inflection after a prolonged period of limited growth, as "demand for gas infrastructure in response to power demand growth, LNG exports, and re-industrialization gain traction," Thakkar wrote.

BMO begins Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) with an Outperform rating and $185 PT, optimistic the company "can still grow through the current lower Permian rig environment and drive EBITDA/FCF growth, and expect TRGP volumes to continue to outpace the basin average."

Williams (NYSE:WMB), rated Outperform with a $66 PT, is "one of the most attractive gas-focused midstream operators with a robust financial outlook offering both growth (BMO estimates 8% EBITDA CAGR 2024-29) and income (3.5% current dividend yield) supported by a stable, fee-based business model and backlog of accretive growth projects."
